title: De-identifying Transcripts with ghosted
text: Speaker labels start line/paragraph normalized Interviewer Participant (e.g., Name: <v Name> → Interviewer:), names elsewhere replaced [REDACTED].
code: # VTT → DOCX ghost_vtt(   filepath    = "path/to/meeting.vtt",   interviewers = "Sansa Stark",   interviewees = "Arya Stark",   out_format   = "docx",            # or "vtt" / "txt"   output_dir   = "output/",   suffix       = "_DEID" )  # DOCX → DOCX ghost_docx(   filepath     = "path/to/transcript.docx",   interviewers = "Sansa Stark",   interviewees = "Arya Stark",   output_path  = "output/transcript_DEID.docx" )  # TXT → TXT ghost_txt(   filepath     = "path/to/notes.txt",   interviewers = "Sansa Stark",   interviewees = "Arya Stark",   output_path  = "output/notes_DEID.txt" )

title: De-identifying Transcripts with ghosted
text: Notes Progress shown console progress bar. DOCX/TXT → VTT conversions (out_format = "vtt"), cues written without timestamps. Install officer DOCX reads/writes.
code: res <- ghost_batch(   input_dir    = "path/to/transcripts",   interviewers = "Sansa Stark",   interviewees = "Arya Stark",   output_dir   = "output/",   suffix       = "_DEID",      # default   out_format   = NULL           # keep each file’s type; or "docx"/"txt"/"vtt" ) print(res)

title: Authors and Citation
text: Abiraahmi Shankar. Author, maintainer. Catalina Canizares. Author. Francisco Cardozo. Author, contributor. Pamela Morris-Perez. Contributor.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Shankar , Canizares C, Cardozo F (2026). ghosted: Deidentify & Batch Process Transcript Files. R package version 0.0.1.9, https://abiraahmi.github.io/ghosted/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {ghosted: Deidentify & Batch Process Transcript Files},   author = {Abiraahmi Shankar and Catalina Canizares and Francisco Cardozo},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.1.9},   url = {https://abiraahmi.github.io/ghosted/}, }

title: Deidentify & Batch Process Transcript Files
text: can install development version ghosted like :
code: # via remotes remotes::install_github("abiraahmi/ghosted")  # or with pak # pak::pak("abiraahmi/ghosted")

title: Deidentify & Batch Process Transcript Files
text: Notes - Leading speaker tokens line start (e.g., Name: <v Name>) normalized Interviewer/Participant names elsewhere redacted. - DOCX/TXT → VTT conversions ghost_batch(out_format = "vtt"), cues written without timestamps. - Ensure officer installed .docx reads/writes.
code: library(ghosted)  # VTT → DOCX (or "vtt"/"txt") ghost_vtt(   filepath     = "inst/data/sample.vtt",   interviewers = "Sansa Stark",   interviewees = "Arya Stark",   out_format   = "docx",   output_dir   = "output",   suffix       = "_DEID" )  # DOCX → DOCX ghost_docx(   filepath     = "inst/data/transcript.docx",   interviewers = "Sansa Stark",   interviewees = "Arya Stark",   output_path  = "output/transcript_DEID.docx" )  # TXT → TXT ghost_txt(   filepath     = "inst/data/notes.txt",   interviewers = "Sansa Stark",   interviewees = "Arya Stark",   output_path  = "output/notes_DEID.txt" )  # Batch a folder (keep file types) res <- ghost_batch(   input_dir    = "inst/data/transcripts",   interviewers = "Sansa Stark",   interviewees = "Arya Stark",   output_dir   = "output",   suffix       = "_DEID" ) print(res)

what: Batch redact transcripts in a folder — ghost_batch
title: Batch redact transcripts in a folder — ghost_batch
text: Scans folder transcript files runs appropriate redaction function per file type: .docx -> ghost_docx() .txt  -> ghost_txt() .vtt  -> ghost_vtt() (can output VTT/DOCX/TXT)

title: Batch redact transcripts in a folder — ghost_batch
code: ghost_batch(   input_dir,   interviewers,   interviewees = character(),   output_dir = NULL,   recursive = FALSE,   suffix = "_redacted",   out_format = NULL,   redact_other = character(),   redact_interviewer = FALSE,   include_common_names = FALSE,   common_names_fun = NULL,   report_redacted = FALSE,   name_token = "[REDACTED]",   school_token = "[REDACTED]",   add_blank_line_between_turns = TRUE )

title: Batch redact transcripts in a folder — ghost_batch
text: input_dir Folder containing transcripts. interviewers Character vector interviewer names. interviewees Character vector interviewee/participant names. output_dir Folder write outputs (default: input_dir). recursive TRUE, include files subdirectories. suffix Suffix append output filename (default: _redacted). out_format Output format inputs; one "vtt", "docx", "txt". NULL (default), file keeps original format. request "vtt" .docx/.txt inputs, cues written without timestamps. redact_other words/phrases redact. redact_interviewer TRUE, also redact interviewer names. include_common_names TRUE, also redact top US baby names (uses common_names_fun). common_names_fun Function used include_common_names = TRUE (default: top 1000 US baby names babynames). report_redacted TRUE, print phrases found/redacted per file. name_token Replacement token names. school_token Replacement token schools/phrases. add_blank_line_between_turns VTT -> DOCX/TXT outputs, insert blank line turns.

title: Batch redact transcripts in a folder — ghost_batch
text: data.frame input_file, output_file, status columns. Invisibly returned.

title: Batch redact transcripts in a folder — ghost_batch
text: Outputs written output_dir (defaults input_dir) using input base filename suffix appended extension. Shows console progress bar processing.

title: Batch redact transcripts in a folder — ghost_batch
code: # Redact all transcripts in a folder, keeping the same formats: # ghost_batch("inst/data/transcripts", interviewers = "Dr. Smith", #   interviewees = "Jane Doe", suffix = "_DEID") # Redact VTTs to DOCX, others keep their formats: # ghost_batch("inst/data/transcripts", interviewers = "Dr. Smith", #   interviewees = "Jane Doe", out_format = "docx")

what: Read a Word file and redact it (no data.frame) — ghost_docx
title: Read a Word file and redact it (no data.frame) — ghost_docx
text: Reads .docx file raw paragraphs, applies -function redaction, writes redacted Word file. speaker/text data.frame created; document treated sequence paragraphs.

title: Read a Word file and redact it (no data.frame) — ghost_docx
code: ghost_docx(   filepath,   interviewers,   interviewees = character(),   output_path = NULL,   redact_other = character(),   redact_interviewer = FALSE,   include_common_names = FALSE,   common_names_fun = NULL,   report_redacted = FALSE,   name_token = "[REDACTED]",   school_token = "[REDACTED]" )

title: Read a Word file and redact it (no data.frame) — ghost_docx
text: filepath Path .docx file. interviewers Character vector interviewer names. interviewees Character vector interviewee/participant names. output_path Path redacted .docx. NULL (default), set directory base name filepath _redacted extension (e.g. report.docx -> report_redacted.docx). redact_other words/phrases redact. redact_interviewer TRUE, also redact interviewer names. include_common_names TRUE, also redact top US baby names (uses common_names_fun). common_names_fun Function used include_common_names = TRUE (default: top 1000 US baby names babynames). report_redacted TRUE, print R console phrases found redacted (names ). name_token Replacement token names. school_token Replacement token schools/phrases.

title: Read a Word file and redact it (no data.frame) — ghost_docx
text: path written .docx file (invisibly).

title: Read a Word file and redact it (no data.frame) — ghost_docx
code: # Writes report_redacted.docx in same folder, returns path: # ghost_docx("report.docx", interviewers = "Dr. Smith", interviewees = "Jane Doe") # With common names and redaction report: # ghost_docx("report.docx", interviewers = "Dr. Smith", interviewees = "Jane Doe", #   include_common_names = TRUE, report_redacted = TRUE)

what: Read a text file and redact it (no data.frame) — ghost_txt
title: Read a text file and redact it (no data.frame) — ghost_txt
text: Reads .txt file line--line, applies -function redaction, writes redacted text file. speaker/text data.frame created; file treated sequence lines.

title: Read a text file and redact it (no data.frame) — ghost_txt
code: ghost_txt(   filepath,   interviewers,   interviewees = character(),   output_path = NULL,   redact_other = character(),   redact_interviewer = FALSE,   include_common_names = FALSE,   common_names_fun = NULL,   report_redacted = FALSE,   name_token = "[REDACTED]",   school_token = "[REDACTED]" )

title: Read a text file and redact it (no data.frame) — ghost_txt
text: filepath Path .txt file. interviewers Character vector interviewer names. interviewees Character vector interviewee/participant names. output_path Path redacted .txt. NULL (default), set directory base name filepath _redacted extension (e.g. notes.txt -> notes_redacted.txt). redact_other words/phrases redact. redact_interviewer TRUE, also redact interviewer names. include_common_names TRUE, also redact top US baby names (uses common_names_fun). common_names_fun Function used include_common_names = TRUE (default: top 1000 US baby names babynames). report_redacted TRUE, print R console phrases found redacted (names ). name_token Replacement token names. school_token Replacement token schools/phrases.

title: Read a text file and redact it (no data.frame) — ghost_txt
text: path written .txt file (invisibly).

title: Read a text file and redact it (no data.frame) — ghost_txt
code: # Writes notes_redacted.txt in same folder, returns path: # ghost_txt("notes.txt", interviewers = "Dr. Smith", interviewees = "Jane Doe") # With common names and redaction report: # ghost_txt("notes.txt", interviewers = "Dr. Smith", interviewees = "Jane Doe", #   include_common_names = TRUE, report_redacted = TRUE)

what: Redact a Zoom/WebVTT transcript and write VTT/DOCX/TXT — ghost_vtt
title: Redact a Zoom/WebVTT transcript and write VTT/DOCX/TXT — ghost_vtt
text: Parses Zoom/WebVTT transcript raw cues (data.frame), redacts interviewee names (optionally interviewer names) plus phrases using boundary-aware matching, writes result WebVTT, Word, plain text file.

title: Redact a Zoom/WebVTT transcript and write VTT/DOCX/TXT — ghost_vtt
code: ghost_vtt(   filepath,   interviewers,   interviewees = character(),   output_dir = NULL,   out_format = c("vtt", "docx", "txt"),   suffix = "_redacted",   redact_other = character(),   redact_interviewer = FALSE,   include_common_names = FALSE,   common_names_fun = NULL,   report_redacted = FALSE,   name_token = "[REDACTED]",   school_token = "[REDACTED]",   add_blank_line_between_turns = TRUE )

title: Redact a Zoom/WebVTT transcript and write VTT/DOCX/TXT — ghost_vtt
text: filepath Path .vtt file. interviewers Character vector interviewer names. interviewees Character vector interviewee/participant names. output_dir Output folder. NULL, uses folder filepath. output filename derived input base name suffix extension based out_format. out_format One "vtt", "docx", "txt" controlling output file extension. suffix Suffix append base filename (default: "_redacted"). redact_other words/phrases redact. redact_interviewer TRUE, also redact interviewer names transcript text. Interviewer names speaker field always redacted. include_common_names TRUE, also redact top US baby names (uses common_names_fun). common_names_fun Function used include_common_names = TRUE (default: top 1000 US baby names babynames). report_redacted TRUE, prints phrases found redacted. name_token Replacement token names. school_token Replacement token schools/phrases. add_blank_line_between_turns Logical; DOCX/TXT outputs, insert blank line turns.

title: Redact a Zoom/WebVTT transcript and write VTT/DOCX/TXT — ghost_vtt
text: Invisibly, output path written.

title: Redact a Zoom/WebVTT transcript and write VTT/DOCX/TXT — ghost_vtt
text: Redaction mirrors standalone logic used ghost_docx() ghost_txt(): full names also split parts (e.g., first/last hyphenated pieces) replaced longest-first tokens.

title: Redact a Zoom/WebVTT transcript and write VTT/DOCX/TXT — ghost_vtt
code: # Write redacted VTT next to source: # ghost_vtt("meeting.vtt", interviewers = "Dr. Smith", interviewees = "Jane Doe") # Write redacted DOCX with report: # ghost_vtt("meeting.vtt", interviewers = "Dr. Smith", interviewees = "Jane Doe", #   out_format = "docx", report_redacted = TRUE)

title: ghosted 0.0.1.8 (2026-03-04)
text: New functions allow different file types: ghost_vtt(), ghost_docx(), ghost_txt(), ghost_batch(). Leading speaker names line start normalized Interviewer/Participant; names elsewhere redacted. ghost_batch() adds progress bar flexible out_format across input types. VTT conversion DOCX/TXT writes cues without timestamps. README vignette updated reflect changes.

title: ghosted 0.0.1.7 (2026-03-03)
text: Added website getting-started vignette.

title: ghosted 0.0.1.6 (2026-03-01)
text: Initial development version.
